cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Turkic Islamic empire (1299–1922) centered in Constantinople that controlled southeastern Europe, the Middle East, and North Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Persian empire (1501–1736) that established Twelver Shia Islam as the state religion

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Islamic empire in South Asia (1526–1857) known for cultural synthesis, the Taj Mahal, and rulers like Akbar

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Manchu-ruled dynasty in China (1644–1912) that expanded territory and maintained Confucian governance while preserving Manchu identity

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ottoman system of conscripting Christian boys from the Balkans, converting them to Islam, and training them as soldiers or administrators

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Elite Ottoman infantry originally recruited through the devshirme system as enslaved Christians

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ottoman policy allowing religious minorities to govern their own internal affairs under their own religious leaders

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Term for the Ottoman, Safavid, and Mughal empires that relied on cannons and muskets to build and maintain power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: System in which the right to collect taxes was sold to private individuals who paid the state a fixed amount and kept any surplus

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mughal tax collectors granted authority over land revenue collection who became powerful local elites

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Akbar's policy of universal peace promoting religious toleration and blending Hindu and Islamic traditions at the Mughal court

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Akbar
cards.answer: Mughal emperor (r. 1556–1605) known for religious toleration, administrative reforms, and his policy of sulh-i-kul (universal peace)
alternate_answers: Mughal emperor who promoted religious tolerance

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Aurangzeb
cards.answer: Mughal emperor (r. 1658–1707) who reimposed the jizya tax and pursued orthodox Islamic policies, contributing to the empire's decline
alternate_answers: Mughal emperor who reversed Akbar's toleration

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Suleiman the Magnificent
cards.answer: Ottoman sultan (r. 1520–1566) who presided over the empire's golden age, expanding territory and patronizing art and legal reform
alternate_answers: Ottoman sultan during the empire's golden age

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Peter the Great
cards.answer: Russian tsar (r. 1682–1725) who westernized Russia through military, administrative, and cultural reforms including building St. Petersburg

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Shah Abbas I
cards.answer: Safavid ruler (r. 1588–1629) who modernized the military, built Isfahan as a grand capital, and strengthened Safavid power at its peak
alternate_answers: Greatest Safavid ruler who built Isfahan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ivan IV (Ivan the Terrible)
cards.answer: First Russian tsar (r. 1547–1584) who centralized power, created the oprichnina secret police, and expanded Russia eastward
alternate_answers: First Russian tsar who centralized power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Kangxi Emperor
cards.answer: Qing emperor (r. 1661–1722) who consolidated Manchu rule, expanded China's borders, and patronized Confucian scholarship and the arts

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ottoman conquest of Constantinople

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Founding of the Safavid Empire by Shah Ismail I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Babur's founding of the Mughal Empire (Battle of Panipat)

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Establishment of the Qing Dynasty by the Manchus

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Reign of Suleiman the Magnificent

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Devshirme
cards.answer: Ottoman system of conscripting Christian boys from the Balkans, converting them to Islam, and training them as janissaries or administrators

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Millet system
cards.answer: Ottoman policy allowing religious minority communities (Christians, Jews) to govern their own internal affairs under their own religious leaders

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Slavic warriors and frontiersmen who aided Russian expansion across Siberia in exchange for land and autonomy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Iconic Mughal mausoleum in Agra built by Shah Jahan for his wife Mumtaz Mahal, blending Islamic and Hindu architectural elements

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Sikhism
cards.answer: Monotheistic religion founded in Punjab (15th century) blending elements of Hinduism and Islam, emphasizing equality and devotion to one God

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Qing policy requiring Chinese men to wear their hair in a long braided queue as a sign of submission to Manchu authority

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Janissaries
cards.answer: Elite Ottoman infantry force recruited through the devshirme system, originally consisting of enslaved Christian boys converted to Islam and trained as loyal soldiers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Grand Safavid capital city rebuilt by Shah Abbas I with magnificent mosques, bazaars, and the famous Naqsh-e Jahan Square

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Babur
cards.answer: Central Asian warrior-prince descended from both Timur and Genghis Khan who founded the Mughal Empire after the Battle of Panipat in 1526

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: System of enserfment in Russia that legally bound peasants to the land of their lords, intensifying as Russian expansion required labor

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Peter the Great's founding of St. Petersburg

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Absolute monarchy
cards.answer: System of governance in which a single ruler holds total power with no constitutional limits, exemplified by rulers like Louis XIV and Peter the Great
